channel.title: Ryan Rasti
channel.copyright: Give me the strength to be better than I was yesterday.

item.title: Why you should rewrite your resume in JSON in 2019
item.author: rrrasti@yahoo.com (Ryan Rasti)
item.description: Why you should rewrite your resume in JSON in 2019 Reposting this from LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/refactor-your-resume-json-2019-ryan-rasti/

item.title: About
item.author: rrrasti@yahoo.com (Ryan Rasti)
item.description: My name is Ryan Rasti, and I am currently a Senior SDET/DevOps at CWTSatoTravel, located in San Antonio, TX. I love spending time with my family, learning new technologies, training in the gym, and playing oldschool video games. I have prior work experience in the web technology space, having previously worked as an IT manager, DevOps and Infrastructure Engineer, and Software Test Engineer. I am also extremely comfortable wearing multiple hats, and thrive in environments where generalists can make a bigger impact than specialists (typically smaller shops or startups).

item.title: How I added SSL to my blog for free with CloudFlare
item.author: rrrasti@yahoo.com (Ryan Rasti)
item.description: How I added SSL to this domain for free Hosting your blog for free on Github pages is a great way to have a web presence without paying hosting fees. It’s secure, in that your most likely deploying a static site, and the responsibility of infrastructure patching and hardening is offloaded to Github (who is most likely better at it than you). However, one thing that is now mandatory, that you don’t get for free with vanilla Github pages is SSL.

item.title: Hugo Hosting Revisited
item.author: rrrasti@yahoo.com (Ryan Rasti)
item.description: New Domain, New Theme, Same Generator Well it was about time I spun up a new site. I still love using Hugo, as it’s combination of rendering speed, local-development friendliness, theme integration and testing, and elegant use of Markdown, make it an obvious choice. Plus, it’s written in Go! I initially gave Wordpress a try, and I still think there is a ton of value on that platform for actual monetization of content and user interaction… but for a mostly personal blog or professional portfolio/contact site, Hugo has it beat.
